get.summary: Get the metadata for all elements in a document.
get.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* You can specify the optional `depth` query parameter to get multiple levels in an assembly. Default `depth` is `1`. 
* `linkDocumentId` can be specified where applicable. Combined with `inferMetadataOwner` (default value is `false`), this is used to infer metadata owner. 
* `includeComputedProperties` can be used to include or omit computed properties. Default value is `true`. 
* `includeComputedAssemblyProperties` can be used to query computed assembly properties which are generally expensive. Default value is `false`. 
* You can also choose to include a `thumbnail`. Default value is `false`.

get.summary: Get the metadata for an element.
get.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* `linkDocumentId` can be specified where applicable and this combined with the query param `inferMetadataOwner` (default value is `false`) will be used to infer metadata owner. 
* `configuration` optional query parameter defaults to default configuration. 
* You can specify an optional `depth` query parameter to get multiple levels in an assembly. Default `depth` is `1`. 
* `includeComputedProperties` can be used to include or omit computed properties. Default value is `true`. 
* `includeComputedAssemblyProperties` can be used to query computed assembly properties which are generally expensive. Default value is `false`. 
* You can also choose to include a `thumbnail`. Default value is `false`.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

post.summary: Update the metadata for an element.
post.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* Microversion (`m`) in `wvm` path parameter option is not supported. 
* Specify the property metadata to update in the Request body.

get.summary: Get the metadata for an assembly, including supporting metadata.
get.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* `linkDocumentId` can be specified where applicable and this combined with the query param `inferMetadataOwner` (default value is `false`) will be used to infer metadata owner. 
* `configuration` optional query parameter defaults to default configuration. 
* `includeComputedProperties` can be used to include or omit computed properties. Default value is `true`. 
* `includeComputedAssemblyProperties` can be used to query computed assembly properties which are generally expensive. Default value is `false`. 
* You can also choose to include a `thumbnail`. Default value is `false`.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

get.summary: Get the metadata for all parts in a document.
get.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* The `configuration` optional query parameter uses the default configuration unless otherwise specified. 
* You can specify an optional `depth` query parameter to get multiple levels in an assembly. Default `depth` is `1`. 
* `linkDocumentId` can be specified where applicable. Combined with `inferMetadataOwner` (default value is `false`), this is used to infer metadata owner. 
* `includeComputedProperties` can be used to include or omit computed properties. Default value is `true`. 
* `includeComputedAssemblyProperties` can be used to query computed assembly properties which are generally expensive. Default value is `false`. 
* You can also choose to include a `thumbnail`. Default value is `false`.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: URL-encoded string of configuration values (separated by `;`). See the [Configurations API Guide](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/configs/) for details.

get.summary: Get the metadata for a part.
get.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* Specify the part in the `iden` or `pid` path parameter. 
* The `configuration` optional query parameter uses the default configuration unless otherwise specified. 
* `linkDocumentId` can be specified where applicable. Combined with `inferMetadataOwner` (default value is `false`), this is used to infer metadata owner. 
* `includeComputedProperties` can be used to include or omit computed properties. Default value is `true`. 
* `includeComputedAssemblyProperties` can be used to query computed assembly properties which are generally expensive. Default value is `false`. 
* You can also choose to include a `thumbnail`. Default value is `false`.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: URL-encoded string of configuration values (separated by `;`). See the [Configurations API Guide](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/configs/) for details.

parameters.name: rollbackBarIndex
parameters.description: Index specifying the location of the rollback bar when the call is evaluated. A -1 indicates that it should be at the end of the featurelist.

parameters.name: elementMicroversionId
parameters.description: A specific element microversion in which to evaluate the request.

parameters.name: iden
parameters.description: Denotes whether the pid specified is a part id (p) or a part identity (pi).

post.summary: Update the metadata for a part.
post.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* Specify the part in the `iden` or `pid` path parameter. 
* The `configuration` optional query parameter uses the default configuration unless otherwise specified. 
* `linkDocumentId` can be specified where applicable. Combined with `inferMetadataOwner` (default value is `false`), this is used to infer metadata owner. 
* Specify the property metadata to update in the Request body.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId

This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: URL-encoded string of configuration values (separated by `;`). See the [Configurations API Guide](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/configs/) for details.

parameters.description: Index specifying the location of the rollback bar when the call is evaluated. A -1 indicates that it should be at the end of the featurelist.

get.summary: Get the metadata for a workspace or version.
get.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* `linkDocumentId` can be specified where applicable. Combined with `inferMetadataOwner` (default value is `false`), this is used to infer metadata owner. 
* You can specify an optional `depth` query parameter to get multiple levels in an assembly. Default `depth` is `1`. 
* `includeComputedProperties` can be used to include or omit computed properties. Default value is `true`. 
* `includeComputedAssemblyProperties` can be used to query computed assembly properties which are generally expensive. Default value is `false`. 
* You can also choose to include a `thumbnail`. Default value is `false`.

post.summary: Update the metadata for a workspace or version.
Update the metadata for a standard content part.
post.description: Specify the property metadata to update in the Request body. See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/#update-standard-content-part-metadata) for an example.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The ID of the document that owns the standard content part.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The ID of the document in which you have inserted the standard content part.

get.summary: Get the metadata for a standard content part.
get.description:
See [API Guide: Metadata](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/metadata/) for details. 
* Specify the part in the `pid` path parameter. 
* The `configuration` and `linkDocumentId` query parameters are required. 
* `includeComputedProperties` can be used to include or omit computed properties. Default value is `true`. 
* `includeComputedAssemblyProperties` can be used to query computed assembly properties which are generally expensive. Default value is `false`. 
* You can also choose to include a `thumbnail`. Default value is `false`.

properties.description: Properties associated with this metadata object

propertyOverrideStatus.description: 0: Unknown | 1: Not computed | 2: Computed without override | 3: Computed with override | 4: Computed with subassembly overrides | 5: Overridden

scopes.OAuth2ReadPII: Application can read your profile information
scopes.OAuth2Write: Application can write to your documents
scopes.OAuth2Delete: Application can delete your documents and workspaces
scopes.OAuth2Purchase: Application can request purchases on your behalf
scopes.OAuth2Share: Application can share and unshare documents on your behalf
scopes.document.create: Atlas Application can create a document
scopes.document.edit: Atlas Application can edit a document
scopes.document.delete: Atlas Application can delete a document
scopes.document.read: Atlas Application can read a document
scopes.enterprise.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.read: Atlas Application can read from an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.edit: Atlas Application can edit an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.delete: Atlas Application can delete an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.member.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.read: Atlas Application can read the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.edit: Atlas Application can edit the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.delete: Atlas Application can delete a member from an enterprise
scopes.webhook.create: Atlas Application can create a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.edit: Atlas Application can edit a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.read: Atlas Application can read a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.delete: Atlas Application can delete a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.PLMIntegration: PLM automation can invoke limited operations

BasicAuth.description: Use Basic Authentication with API Keys (key as username and secret as password) to authenticate requests.
